text: Can you give me a discount?
What is your price per ton?
Can you give me a discount for a bigger order?
The total is more important than the price per ton.
I need 30 days to pay after the invoice.

Hi Zilaudio, this is Mark from Grainline in Chicago. I am calling about the quote I sent you this morning. I want to give you our best price. The price is one thousand nine hundred reais per ton, but for orders over one thousand tons, I can add a discount of two percent. The delivery is free, in fourteen days. And you have thirty days to pay after the invoice. Please compare our quote with the others, and call me back before Friday. Thank you, and talk to you soon.

Good morning, and here is a short reminder about your two quotes. Before you choose, check three things on every quote. First, the price per ton, and remember that the cheapest price is not always the best deal. Second, the delivery: is it free, and how many days? Third, the payment: how much time do you have to pay after the invoice? Look at the total, not only the price per ton. If the delivery is free and the payment time is longer, a higher price can still be the better offer. Have a good week.
Grainline is more expensive than Riverport.
Free delivery is better than paid delivery.
The price is 1,900 reais per ton.
You get a discount for orders over 1,000 tons.
1,850 is under 1,900, so it is cheaper.
What is your price per ton?
Can you give me a discount for a bigger order?
The total is more important than the price per ton.
I need 30 days to pay after the invoice.
